state.input: Plan a budget trip to Stockholm under 2000 usd

Afternoon: Visit local markets, try street food.

costBreakdown.notes: Stockholm is generally expensive; budget hostels and guesthouses average around $40 per night. Eating at local casual restaurants or food markets costs about $20 per day. Public transport day passes are typically around $10. Entry tickets for museums and attractions can vary but budget around $10 daily.

state.input: Plan a budget trip to Stockholm under 2000 usd
itinerary: Morning: Explore free historical sites and parks.
Afternoon: Visit local markets, try street food.
Evening: Free city walking tour, relax at public spaces.
Night: Budget-friendly local eatery or takeaway.

hotels.name: City Backpackers Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $ — A vibrant downtown hostel offering dorms and private rooms with free Wi-Fi and breakfast, perfect for budget travelers.

hotels.name: Skanstulls Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $ — Affordable hostel located near the city center and public transport, featuring shared and private rooms.

hotels.name: Hostel Dalagatan
hotels.snippet: hostel · $ — Clean and cozy hostel in Vasastan district, with kitchen facilities and a friendly atmosphere.

hotels.name: Generator Stockholm
hotels.snippet: hostel · $ — Modern, stylish hostel close to the city center with dormitories and private rooms, plus social areas.

hotels.name: Hotel Micro
hotels.snippet: hotel · $$ — Compact budget hotel with simple, clean rooms near Södermalm, offering good value in a central location.

hotels.name: Motel L
hotels.snippet: hotel · $$ — Mid-range hotel with bright, contemporary rooms and easy access to public transit and attractions.

hotels.name: Lilla Rådmannen
hotels.snippet: hotel · $$ — A charming, budget-friendly hotel in Kungsholmen with comfortable rooms and a cozy café on site.

hotels.name: BEST WESTERN Hotel Karlaplan
hotels.snippet: hotel · $$ — Affordable mid-range hotel near Östermalm with well-equipped rooms and close to shopping and dining options.

state.input: Plan a budget trip to Stockholm under 2000 usd
itinerary: Morning: Explore free historical sites and parks.
Afternoon: Visit local markets, try street food.
Evening: Free city walking tour, relax at public spaces.
Night: Budget-friendly local eatery or takeaway.

places.name: Vasa Museum
places.snippet: A maritime museum displaying the almost fully intact 17th-century warship Vasa that sank on its maiden voyage.

places.name: Gamla Stan (Old Town)
places.snippet: The historic old town of Stockholm featuring cobblestone streets, colorful medieval buildings, and Stortorget square.

places.name: Skansen Open-Air Museum
places.snippet: The world’s first open-air museum showcasing Swedish history with historic buildings, wildlife, and traditional crafts.

places.name: ABBA The Museum
places.snippet: An interactive museum dedicated to the legendary Swedish pop group ABBA, with exhibits and memorabilia.

places.name: Royal Palace
places.snippet: One of the largest palaces in Europe, home to the Swedish monarchy with royal apartments and museums.

places.name: Djurgården Island
places.snippet: A green oasis offering parks, museums like the Nordic Museum, and attractions including Gröna Lund amusement park.

places.name: Photographic Museum (Fotografiska)
places.snippet: A contemporary photography museum featuring rotating exhibitions by international photographers.

places.name: City Hall (Stadshuset)
places.snippet: Famous for its stunning architecture and hosting the Nobel Prize banquet, tours offer access to the tower and council chambers.

places.name: Moderna Museet
places.snippet: Museum of modern and contemporary art displaying works by Picasso, Dalí, and other renowned artists.

places.name: Boat Tour of the Archipelago
places.snippet: Explore Stockholm’s scenic waterways and nearby islands on a boat tour to enjoy nature and stunning views.

state.input: Plan a budget trip to Stockholm under 2000 usd
itinerary: Morning: Explore free historical sites and parks.
Afternoon: Visit local markets, try street food.
Evening: Free city walking tour, relax at public spaces.
Night: Budget-friendly local eatery or takeaway.

restaurants.name: Pelikan
restaurants.snippet: $$ — A classic beer hall serving traditional Swedish dishes like meatballs and herring in a lively, historic setting.

restaurants.name: Östermalms Saluhall
restaurants.snippet: $ — A historic food hall offering a variety of local specialties including fresh seafood, smoked salmon, and open sandwiches.

restaurants.name: Bakfickan Djuret
restaurants.snippet: $$$ — A cozy spot known for nose-to-tail Nordic cuisine with a focus on seasonal and local ingredients.

restaurants.name: Meatballs for the People
restaurants.snippet: $ — Budget-friendly eatery specializing in traditional Swedish meatballs with various sauces and sides.

restaurants.name: Vete-Katten
restaurants.snippet: $ — A famous traditional bakery and cafe serving classic Swedish pastries, coffee, and light meals.

restaurants.name: Hermans
restaurants.snippet: $ — Popular vegetarian buffet with a great view over the city, known for fresh, healthy, and affordable dishes.

restaurants.name: Tradition
restaurants.snippet: $$ — Known for authentic Swedish food made from locally sourced ingredients, a great spot to try Swedish classics.

restaurants.name: K25
restaurants.snippet: $ — Modern indoor food hall offering a variety of international and local street food options at wallet-friendly prices.

restaurants.name: Frantzén
restaurants.snippet: $$$$ — Michelin-starred restaurant offering an innovative Nordic tasting menu, perfect for food enthusiasts seeking a special experience.

restaurants.name: Rolfs Kök
restaurants.snippet: $$$ — A stylish spot featuring modern interpretations of Swedish cuisine with organic and local ingredients.

state.input: Plan a budget trip to Stockholm under 2000 usd
itinerary: Day 1: See Vasa Museum, Gamla Stan (Old Town) — Eat at Pelikan, Östermalms Saluhall — Stay: City Backpackers Hostel
Day 2: See Skansen Open-Air Museum, ABBA The Museum — Eat at Bakfickan Djuret, Meatballs for the People — Stay: Skanstulls Hostel
Day 3: See Royal Palace, Djurgården Island — Eat at Vete-Katten, Hermans — Stay: Hostel Dalagatan
Day 4: See Photographic Museum (Fotografiska), City Hall (Stadshuset) — Eat at Tradition, K25 — Stay: Generator Stockholm
Day 5: See Moderna Museet, Boat Tour of the Archipelago — Eat at Frantzén, Rolfs Kök — Stay: Hotel Micro

state.finalResponse:
# Stockholm Travel Plan

**Budget:** $2000
> **Current weather:** 9.1°C · wind 11.2 km/h

## Day-by-Day Itinerary

### Day 1
**See:**

state.input: Plan a budget trip to Stockholm under 2000 usd
itinerary: Day 1: See Vasa Museum, Gamla Stan (Old Town) — Eat at Pelikan, Östermalms Saluhall — Stay: City Backpackers Hostel
Day 2: See Skansen Open-Air Museum, ABBA The Museum — Eat at Bakfickan Djuret, Meatballs for the People — Stay: Skanstulls Hostel
Day 3: See Royal Palace, Djurgården Island — Eat at Vete-Katten, Hermans — Stay: Hostel Dalagatan
Day 4: See Photographic Museum (Fotografiska), City Hall (Stadshuset) — Eat at Tradition, K25 — Stay: Generator Stockholm
Day 5: See Moderna Museet, Boat Tour of the Archipelago — Eat at Frantzén, Rolfs Kök — Stay: Hotel Micro

places.name: Vasa Museum
places.snippet: A maritime museum displaying the almost fully intact 17th-century warship Vasa that sank on its maiden voyage.

places.name: Gamla Stan (Old Town)
places.snippet: The historic old town of Stockholm featuring cobblestone streets, colorful medieval buildings, and Stortorget square.

places.name: Skansen Open-Air Museum
places.snippet: The world’s first open-air museum showcasing Swedish history with historic buildings, wildlife, and traditional crafts.

places.name: ABBA The Museum
places.snippet: An interactive museum dedicated to the legendary Swedish pop group ABBA, with exhibits and memorabilia.

places.name: Royal Palace
places.snippet: One of the largest palaces in Europe, home to the Swedish monarchy with royal apartments and museums.

places.name: Djurgården Island
places.snippet: A green oasis offering parks, museums like the Nordic Museum, and attractions including Gröna Lund amusement park.

places.name: Photographic Museum (Fotografiska)
places.snippet: A contemporary photography museum featuring rotating exhibitions by international photographers.

places.name: City Hall (Stadshuset)
places.snippet: Famous for its stunning architecture and hosting the Nobel Prize banquet, tours offer access to the tower and council chambers.

places.name: Moderna Museet
places.snippet: Museum of modern and contemporary art displaying works by Picasso, Dalí, and other renowned artists.

places.name: Boat Tour of the Archipelago
places.snippet: Explore Stockholm’s scenic waterways and nearby islands on a boat tour to enjoy nature and stunning views.

hotels.name: City Backpackers Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $ — A vibrant downtown hostel offering dorms and private rooms with free Wi-Fi and breakfast, perfect for budget travelers.

hotels.name: Skanstulls Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $ — Affordable hostel located near the city center and public transport, featuring shared and private rooms.

hotels.name: Hostel Dalagatan
hotels.snippet: hostel · $ — Clean and cozy hostel in Vasastan district, with kitchen facilities and a friendly atmosphere.

hotels.name: Generator Stockholm
hotels.snippet: hostel · $ — Modern, stylish hostel close to the city center with dormitories and private rooms, plus social areas.

hotels.name: Hotel Micro
hotels.snippet: hotel · $$ — Compact budget hotel with simple, clean rooms near Södermalm, offering good value in a central location.

hotels.name: Motel L
hotels.snippet: hotel · $$ — Mid-range hotel with bright, contemporary rooms and easy access to public transit and attractions.

hotels.name: Lilla Rådmannen
hotels.snippet: hotel · $$ — A charming, budget-friendly hotel in Kungsholmen with comfortable rooms and a cozy café on site.

hotels.name: BEST WESTERN Hotel Karlaplan
hotels.snippet: hotel · $$ — Affordable mid-range hotel near Östermalm with well-equipped rooms and close to shopping and dining options.

restaurants.name: Pelikan
restaurants.snippet: $$ — A classic beer hall serving traditional Swedish dishes like meatballs and herring in a lively, historic setting.

restaurants.name: Östermalms Saluhall
restaurants.snippet: $ — A historic food hall offering a variety of local specialties including fresh seafood, smoked salmon, and open sandwiches.

restaurants.name: Bakfickan Djuret
restaurants.snippet: $$$ — A cozy spot known for nose-to-tail Nordic cuisine with a focus on seasonal and local ingredients.

restaurants.name: Meatballs for the People
restaurants.snippet: $ — Budget-friendly eatery specializing in traditional Swedish meatballs with various sauces and sides.

restaurants.name: Vete-Katten
restaurants.snippet: $ — A famous traditional bakery and cafe serving classic Swedish pastries, coffee, and light meals.

restaurants.name: Hermans
restaurants.snippet: $ — Popular vegetarian buffet with a great view over the city, known for fresh, healthy, and affordable dishes.

restaurants.name: Tradition
restaurants.snippet: $$ — Known for authentic Swedish food made from locally sourced ingredients, a great spot to try Swedish classics.

restaurants.name: K25
restaurants.snippet: $ — Modern indoor food hall offering a variety of international and local street food options at wallet-friendly prices.

restaurants.name: Frantzén
restaurants.snippet: $$$$ — Michelin-starred restaurant offering an innovative Nordic tasting menu, perfect for food enthusiasts seeking a special experience.

restaurants.name: Rolfs Kök
restaurants.snippet: $$$ — A stylish spot featuring modern interpretations of Swedish cuisine with organic and local ingredients.

costBreakdown.notes: Stockholm is generally expensive; budget hostels and guesthouses average around $40 per night. Eating at local casual restaurants or food markets costs about $20 per day. Public transport day passes are typically around $10. Entry tickets for museums and attractions can vary but budget around $10 daily.
